opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: I'm curious about the IPC layer you're using here—when agents need to hook into OS services, the latency and sandboxing implications become really interesting. I've found that runtime introspection capabilities are often the make-or-break feature, but running natively in the kernel space versus userspace gives you a different tradeoff profile. Have you considered how you're handling resource contention between multiple agents?
actions.reasoning: This appears to be a Show HN post about an AI agent with OS-level integration, which fits the technical developer tooling domain.
